topic.what: Unified framework addressing privacy security misinformation and adversarial robustness in federated LLM training
topic.how: Combines gradient smartification with Paillier encryption median Byzantine filtering contrastive grounding and adversarial fine-tuning
topic.why_matters: Existing solutions address LLM safety aspects in isolation creating incompatible defenses and unclear interactions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of harmful content correctly identified across 7 categories
characteristics.context: Full SafeLM vs FedAvg ablation shows no accuracy degradation from safety modules

characteristics.what_it_measures: Peak signal-to-noise ratio of reconstructed gradients from inversion attacks
characteristics.context: Below 20 dB recovery threshold renders reconstruction unrecognizable

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction in per-round bandwidth compared to full-precision FedAvg
characteristics.context: 32x compression via median-threshold binarization to single bit per gradient

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of training labels recoverable from gradient inversion
Relative reduction in hallucinated claims on CNN/DailyMail
characteristics.context: From 34.7% to 20.5% hallucination rate with Misinformation Guard

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack success rate with 20% malicious clients
characteristics.context: Coordinate-wise median filtering limits poisoning effect

characteristics.evidence: Table 4 AdvGLUE 14177 adversarial examples 5 NLU tasks
characteristics.context: Outperforms standalone adversarial training by 3.1 pp under non-IID partitioning

characteristics.what_it_measures: Communication rounds needed to reach 98% accuracy IID K=50
characteristics.context: Only 0.7% overhead versus full-precision FedAvg with 32x compression

characteristics.what_it_measures: Alignment between smartified and full-precision gradients
characteristics.context: Yields ~15% convergence slowdown versus 1/gamma theoretical factor
47.7% relative improvement via Misinformation Guard with calibrated decoding

good_for: federated LLM fine-tuning with strict privacy requirements
high-stakes deployments requiring multiple safety guarantees
scenarios with potential malicious or Byzantine clients
not_for: Applications without computational budget for homomorphic encryption
Settings requiring formal differential privacy epsilon-delta guarantees
requires: NLI entailment classifier for Misinformation Guard
IID or non-IID client data distribution

novelty: Unified framework jointly optimizing four LLM safety pillars
Median-threshold adaptive binarization for gradient smartification
IND-CPA privacy guarantee combining smartification with Paillier encryption
Contrastive grounding with FaithScore hallucination detection
open_problems: Formal convergence guarantees for non-convex LLM optimization under heterogeneous FL
External NLI model bias in Misinformation Guard may propagate
Multilingual safety properties require separate investigation
Bounded reconstruction quality under advanced inversion attacks
